---
title: "Tutorial: Human and Mouse Walkthrough"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Tutorial: Human and Mouse Walkthrough}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

This tutorial uses the tiny built-in tables so the package remains small.

## Human

```{r}
library(expranno)

human <- example_expranno_data("human")
human_anno <- annotate_expr(human$expr, annotation_engine = "none")
human_merged <- merge_expr_meta(human_anno$expr_anno, human$meta)

human_anno$expr_anno
head(human_merged)
```

## Mouse

```{r}
mouse <- example_expranno_data("mouse")
mouse_anno <- annotate_expr(mouse$expr, annotation_engine = "none")
mouse_merged <- merge_expr_meta(mouse_anno$expr_anno, mouse$meta)

mouse_anno$expr_anno
head(mouse_merged)
```

## Next step

To move from the toy workflow to a real workflow:

1. install `biomaRt` and the relevant `OrgDb`
2. rerun `annotate_expr()` with `annotation_engine = "hybrid"`
3. add `immunedeconv` and `GSVA` only if you need deconvolution or signature
   scoring
